explanation: Adding a single trailing whitespace at the end of the command line causes the 'endswith' condition in the Sigma rule to fail, allowing the execution to bypass detection.
Appending an arbitrary or ignored argument to the command line ensures the string no longer terminates with '--Module 1', breaking the strict 'endswith' string matching logic.
Renaming the 'TeamViewer_Desktop.exe' binary to an arbitrary name like 'tv_desktop.exe' bypasses the explicit 'Image' and 'CommandLine' string matches defined in the rule.
